note: Skill -> governing attribute + what it covers. Modifier for a check = attribute mod + skill level + situational/gear bonuses. `does` is the disambiguating description shown in the UI AND fed to the narrator so it picks the right skill.
hazardNote: `hazard: true` means failing this skill can physically HURT you (it's a save against bodily harm — a fall, a crash, a vacuum breach, taking a hit). Only these skills carry failure damage; ability checks (perception, negotiation, mechanics…) never deal damage on a fail — they just fail. Mirrors D&D: ability checks don't cost HP, saving throws do.

piloting.does: flying and maneuvering a ship — dogfights, evasion, hard burns, docking under pressure

zeroG.does: moving, climbing, and fighting in zero-gravity or vacuum (spacewalks, boarding) — NOT plotting courses

gunnery.does: firing ship-mounted weapons in space combat

smallArms.does: personal ranged weapons — pistols, rifles, sidearms

melee.does: close-quarters fighting — blades, fists, improvised weapons

athletics.does: physical feats — forcing/lifting/moving heavy things, climbing, vaulting, breaking through (NOT fighting; that's melee)

stealth.does: moving unseen, hiding, tailing, sleight of hand

streetwise.does: working the underworld — contacts, rumors, fences, black markets, reading a station

mechanics.does: repairing and jury-rigging machinery, engines, and hulls

electronics.does: hacking, slicing, wiring, security systems, sensors, comms

navigation.does: astrogation — plotting FTL JUMPS and lanes, reading star charts, not getting lost

survival.does: enduring hostile environments, field first-aid, staying alive off the grid

perception.does: noticing details — spotting ambushes, traps, tells, hidden things

scavenging.does: salvaging and looting — stripping wrecks and bodies for value, spotting the good parts, appraising a haul. ALWAYS the skill for looting/scavenging.

attackModifierNote: Ship gunnery and small arms roll d20 + reflex mod + skill level; gear (sensor suite, nav computer, thruster overhaul) adds situational modifiers tracked on the ship/character.
